title: Trema rugosum
taxonomy: Trema rugosum is classified in the family Salicaceae. It belongs to the genus Trema, which is a group of tropical trees and shrubs. The species Trema rugosum is native to tropical Asia. Trema rugosum is often found in coastal areas and can thrive in a variety of environments. This species is commonly known as the beach trevalla or beach lead tree.
uses: Trema rugosum is known for its various medicinal uses, primarily in traditional medicine. The plant's leaves and bark are used in the treatment of various ailments, including fever, cough, and skin conditions. In some cultures, the plant is used to treat digestive issues and as an antiseptic. The plant's properties are also said to aid in reducing inflammation and acting as an antioxidant. However, further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Trema rugosum for medicinal purposes.
benefits: Trema rugosum is known to have various health benefits due to its unique properties. The plant contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body. It is also believed to have antimicrobial properties, which can aid in the prevention of infections and promote wound healing. Additionally, Trema rugosum has been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal problems. More research is needed to confirm the full extent of its health benefits.
properties: Trema rugosum has been studied for its potential therapeutic properties, including antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant has been traditionally used in some cultures to treat various health issues, such as fever and skin conditions. constituents: Trema rugosum is a plant species known to contain various active constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins. parts: Trema rugosum is a plant species known for its medicinal properties. The leaves and roots of the plant are typically used in traditional medicine. In some cultures, the leaves are used to treat fever, diarrhea, and other gastrointestinal issues. The roots are often used to treat various ailments, including skin conditions and respiratory problems. preparations: Trema rugosum, also known as Liana, is used in herbal preparations due to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The leaves of Trema rugosum are often dried and used to make teas or infusions, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's bark is also used to make a decoction, which is said to have expectorant properties, helping to relieve coughs and congestion. Additionally, the plant's extracts are sometimes used in modern medicine to treat conditions such as hypertension and diabetes.
intro: Trema rugosum, also known as Lungan or Malabang, is a tropical plant species belonging to the family Cannabaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, thanks to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antipyretic properties, which contribute to its health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Trema rugosum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat various health conditions, particularly in Southeast Asian and Pacific Island traditional medicine.
